mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals imminent labor market slack and recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for return difference falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation and restrictive policy rates create stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger rapid capital outflows, producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 5-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or yields negative returns during confirmed expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirms a technical recession, forcing equity markets to price in falling corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of confirmed recession onsets, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous industrial output expansion and disinflation signals supply chain normalization and easing cost pressures, prompting rapid risk-on positioning and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term lacks out-of-sample predictive stability across rolling windows.
